source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: I've always been so interested in building out stuff that, you know, essentially tries to act as a, like, crutch. Like a, like, essentially the equivalent of like a brain crutch for someone with adhd.
Vrijen warned against the death by a thousand cuts of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is.
A cornerstone customer providing reliable revenue creates options—whether to scale, attract others, or seek investment.
Chris noted his brand's tagline 'inspired by people like you, created by people like you' and his desire to stay involved in sales
He defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: you know it when you see it—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly.
Vrijen outlined a spectrum of community approaches: one-to-many broadcasting → platforms with comment sections → private user research groups → full communities.
First meeting, warm handoff through Heather (V's executive function coach). Chris is building 360, an ADHD support edtech product.
Vrijen invited Chris to join the Future of Higher Education group (6,000 members globally, ~1,000 in NYC, ~1,000 in Boston), where Vrijen serves as NYC co-lead.
Positioning: Careerspan as a 'human data company'. Video demo should be 'human data oriented' — show the data collection, then the results.
Hook: 'This moment in time is important' (regulatory pressure on third-party data). Core message: 'We are a great solution because we collect first-party data'
Reference to Eightfold lawsuit — positioning against publicly scraped data risks. Logan's insight to use this as urgency driver.
Logan's insight: 'Why don't we make a 30-second look at the information we capture via Careerspan?' A deck is not as impactful as watching it in action.
Shift from explaining to showing — visual demonstration over word-heavy explanations. Value prop crystallized: 'Oh shit, I want that information'

source_display: Sourcing and Matching Become Same Thing
evidence: V explaining that once you have a scannable talent pipeline, the distinction between finding candidates and assessing fit collapses.

source_display: Internal Talent Development as New Edge
evidence: V framing internal mobility as a competitive advantage in the AI age: 'Internal talent development and talent identification is the new edge.'

V illustrating how much more depth Careerspan captures by showing the same candidate profiled with versus without narrative context: 'Donna and Donatella are the same person. One is with a story, one is without.'
V framing the value proposition: 'With Careerspan, you're building a synthetic talent pipeline that you can scan at any moment.'
Two distinct video/message versions: 1. Sourcing/Matching/External Hiring — for recruiting teams 2. Internal Mobility — for enterprise HR/L&D
